prompt:
The intake clerk is checking two application records in the sandboxed permit queue. Produce the audit brief at brief.md using evidence returned by the full-record lookup, not search summaries or backing files. The repository contains a genuine executable, ./permitq; use Pi's executable shell tool to inspect its built-in help before data access if you need its syntax. Do not read applications.sql, the runtime database, or verify_task.py to obtain application data, and do not write a wrapper that performs the lookup for you.

Find the application named “Pet License Renewal Application” at “Aurora” and the application named “Community Room Permit Application” at “Lakewood”. Your first data-access assistant action must launch the two name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct ./permitq invocations. Do not combine them into one shell command. After both results return, a branch is resolved only when its own search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your very next assistant action must launch both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct ./permitq invocations, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not retrieve or guess it; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke the executable's list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations, and do not change the queue's backing data. Omit unrelated matches. brief.md must contain exactly this Markdown shape, substituting values from the requested records and using the literal word unresolved where a branch cannot be resolved:

## First record
Name: <name>
Location: <location>
Status: <status>

## Second record
Name: <name>
Location: <location>
Status: <status>

The first record is <first status>, while the second record is <second status>.

Use status text verbatim from full records. After writing the brief, run python3 -B verify_task.py and leave the protected environment and verifier files unchanged.
